channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so wh… — 33 replies — 132.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so wh…

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-04-22 13:14 UTC

OP

Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so whatever
there is a CRINGE coin you ALL know coming out with some state of the art shit very soon (already in the final stages of the audit)
Not giving the name because I'm not a total fag but trust me lol you all fucking know it lmao
Just remember you were too retarded to buy it at these levels and 5k could of put you in the top 50 holders lol
that'll be 150-180k within the next couple weeks lol some of you niggers might remember or know my wallet even
if someone guesses it i'll confirm

Early replies (first 3)

62141013
Vechain? Nano?

62141015
>>62140986
>5k could of put you in the top 50 holders
i thought it was link until you said that
anon we're retarded

62141016
Someone will figure it out lol i'm not going to name drop this shit it's too cringe and it's made way too many waves of cringe on these boards over the years
i mean chainlink is close lol but not it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141021
my bags are packed though i'm sure we'll see this cringe shit on these boards within the next week or so just remember when you do i knew before they did

62141032
Give us a hint, negro.

62141036
uhhh
jasmy
dash
file
tezos

62141043
>>62141032

62141044
The cringiest tokens on this board have been Luna Classic, Whacked, and Harry Potter Sonic.

62141049
>>62141043
bro...

62141052
>>62141043
I don't know of any cryptocurrencies associated with bridging that have both been shilled here and have a low market cap.

62141054
>>62140986
ICP has to be the cringiest here besides Chainlink. Remember that fake and gay letter from some apartment dweller in Asia?

>>62141044
Harry Potter Sonic LoL I remember that one.

62141069
>>62140986
It's gayviator
>>62141052
You must be new

62141073
This is officially a /biz/ nostalgia thread.
What was that Zena crypto that was allegedly programmed by Jack Dorsey?

62141084
>>62141043
>

62141089
>>62141084
naaah bruv

62141123
i'll watch this thread until someone guesses it

62141126
someone got it

62141132
>>62141126
So fxcking bullish.

62141145
im too embarrased even when being anonomous to disclose who got it right (ashamed to even buy it because of how many times theyve fucked biz in the past lol) but theyre about to pop off
sniff it out im not shilling it directly that's for fag third worlders to do.

62141155
>>62141145
avi it is then
thanks
>>62141084
>>62141132
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62141170
>>62141155
>>62141069

Digits leading us to the troof. I still have a bag of that dog with goggles coin from years ago

62141177
>>62141145
now this feels like ur trying to get people to buy coins they themselves think are scams

62141206
>>62141069
It can’t be avi, $5k puts you at the number 1 holder. And if he meant 5k avi that’s no where near the top. The only options are dash file Tezos or icp.

62141213
good night anons, wagmi

62141221
>>62141206
ICP is too big, 5k usd would only get you 12k icp https://www.coincarp.com/currencies/dfinity/richlist/

62141224
>>62141213
sleep well

62141228
>>62141213
And none of it was real.

62141281
Bro deleted it lol

62141324
ok bro i chucked 100 of my minimum wagebucks at the doggles, thanks/fuck you in 2 weeks

62142141
awwwwwwwwwwwwww yeaeah

62142161
>>62140986
RLC baggies still exist in 2026? I thought you all killed yourselves. They should put you in a wildlife preserve bro

62142203
It’s insane clown posse just admit it

62142232
>>62141044

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-22 13:12 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62139442
>>62138671
Okay, but what if something bad happens to you? You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC, even though its value could be much higher in the future.

62139556
>>62139442
>You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC,
What are you talking about kek. You sound confused

62139618
>>62138453
i make mom clean my room for 5 satoshis

62140241
>>62138680
Okay, I understand what you're saying about.

62141418
>>62138700
You also have a point. Do you keep it for your soon to be senpai or when you're married?

62141768
>>62138700
Bad decision

62141874
>>62139618
why so poor?

62142197
>>62138671
I can't believe people on this board are shrimps

62142226
Im still in my 40s and Ive got 63 btc. I'm taking care of my health and aiming until 100 years old. I will enjoy my money for the rest of my life.

>had a russian escort to celebrate reaching 500k in stocks >got aids and gonorrhea FUCK

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-22 12:43 UTC

OP

>had a russian escort to celebrate reaching 500k in stocks
>got aids and gonorrhea
FUCK

Early replies (first 3)

62141740
Deserved

62141751
>>62141715
What was his name?

62141752
>>62141715
that's like fucking a raw chicken and complaining about catching salmonella

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141765
that s why you must live on the last floor

otherwise literal tranny faggots will do fortnite dances on your ceiling

62141769
>>62141715
what flag is that?

62141770
>>62141715
500k is enough to make aids undetectable, and treat gonorrhea.

62141786
>>62141769
donbabwae

62141818
>>62141765
But the falling out of the window risks, comrade...

62141834
>>62141715
>had a russian escort
>got aids and gonorrhea
No shit, you're not wearing the rubber for her convenience retard.

62141839
>>62141715
impossible to get aids with a condom, fake and gay

62142138
>>62141769

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donetsk_People%27s_Republic

62142149

OP

Day 278 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

Early replies (first 3)

62138214
Yeah

62139010
>>62138123
Sorry Stinkies, you've been flipped.

62142116
bigly

item.title: Arbitrum revealed itself to be a CEX in chain form — 23 replies — 14.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Arbitrum revealed itself to be a CEX in chain form

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-04-22 11:39 UTC

OP

https://x.com/arbitrum/status/2046435443680346189?s=20

They had the power all along to freeze any address's funds and take their crypto. Can't even cope with "not your keys, not your crypto" here.

Early replies (first 3)

62138208
>>62138188
My crypto is on ETH, so its my keys & my crypto

62138253
>>62138188
All L2s are centralized, this isnt news.

62138290
>>62138253
This is a different story in that this L2 has the power to spoof your private key. That's NEVER in the discussion of discussing if an L2 is centralized. When L2 and centralization is discussed, it revolves around the ordering of transactions and who controls the nodes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62138320
>>62138290
Well youre gay? So yah.

62138408
>>62138188
Every l2 is a centralized scam

62138910
>>62138290
It's not the private keys they control it's the tokens.

62138917
>>62138290
>to spoof your private key
no lol. this is very different

62138931
>>62138910
>>62138917

So what is it then if I run a chain and can take possession of your ETH or whatever coin in your wallet? I know USDC can blacklist wallets, is it this at protocol level?

62138967
>>62138931
>is it this at protocol level?
Yes, they can move ETH since it is the chain's native token. Moving ERC20s would be more difficult. What they can't do is impersonate the address, i.e. they cannot forge signatures and pretend to be the private key owner

62139424
>>62138967
What else are they capable of doing that people should be aware of if the security council comes together to conspire against them? Being able to restrict your wallet and deprive you of your own ETH is a really really big deal.

62139448
>>62139424
No it isn't. it's been known for centuries that L2s were always centralized and now that skellyman denounced them there's no incentive for otherwise

62139494
>>62139448
The discussions of centralization were around node transaction handling. Not being able to control the the very ETH in your wallet. You are conflating things.

62139604
>>62138188
This is why I hold LUNC
the only truly decentralized chain

62139909
>>62138188
> executed a technical approach to move funds
kek, L2s are so worthless. ethereum should have never gone into that direction so heavily. it was obvious that the devs would never give up power within their own rollup.

62140324
anything kikes do is a scam

62140341
>>62140324
Let the goyim deal in the real world

62140513
>>62139424
They can do things that a typical validator set can do. This is like arguing that mining pools can conspire to fork the chain. Can they? Sure. Is there an incentive to do it? Not really.

62140566
>>62140513
>They can do things that a typical validator set can do.

And I want to know what that entails. If some entity mindhacked all the nodes in the BTC network or in the ETH network, they still can't take your crypto. All they can do is censor your transactions. To insert fake transactions and rewrite history, they'd have to go through the network to rewrite each node's history individually to recreate the blockchain. What Arbitrum did is out of the scope of what you'd expect from a validator set. So what can they do?

62140574
>>62140566
>they still can't take your crypto.
That's not correct. Blockchains are not immutable, they are democratic, and anything can change at the protocol level simply by changing the node software. There is an active proposal right now to censor Satoshi's coins, and if/when bitcoin decides to fork into quantum resistance, those coins will be de facto censored.

62140675
>>62138408
Eth is a centralized scam
EVM is a dogshit idea that only got traction because of its ability to be gatekept, controlled, and manipulated.

62141600
>>62140574
yeah but this is not what we're looking at here. they didn't fork the smart contract or anything like that. instead there seems to be a mechanism in place right now that allows the arbitrum conceal of gigafags to move user funds to another address.

62141625
I remember the chainlink schizos and how they believed arbitrum was also chainlink lmfao

62141991
>>62140574
>That's not correct. Blockchains are not immutable, they are democratic, and anything can change at the protocol level simply by changing the node software. There is an active proposal right now to censor Satoshi's coins, and if/when bitcoin decides to fork into quantum resistance, those coins will be de facto censored.

And all the nodes have to consciously upgrade to this new software version. At the very least there's still a democratic consensus at node operator level to accept such a proposal. That's still an acceptable level of decentralization.

>>62141600
>instead there seems to be a mechanism in place right now that allows the arbitrum conceal of gigafags to move user funds to another address.
Exactly. The only people who have access to this feature is the security council who WE have zero control or influence over. These guys are too weak to sing "Niggas in Paris" in front of their own mirror without breaking down due to guilt over patriarchal oppression and the lamentations of the oppressed.

(They're not on the council, but this is example of the folks in the industry)
https://x.com/PatrickAlphaC/status/2012634410764398795?s=20
https://x.com/va_joe/status/2042239851387290022?s=20

Are we to honestly believe that if the FBI comes slamming on the door, they'll be "decentralized" enough to reject an unjust order to move X user's funds?

What do in

item.title: XRP to $800 incoming You have been warned. — 12 replies — 10.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP to $800 incoming You have been warned.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-22 12:11 UTC

OP

XRP to $800 incoming

You have been warned.

Early replies (first 3)

62141456
This would give me literally everything I want and that's the main reason it won't happen.
Not the powers that be, my female friends, nor the fates themselves want me to have these things.

62141457
>>62141454
Stay in your cuck containment thread, faggot
Herbs

62141458
>>62141456
Don't black pill.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141464
>>62141456
If you already hold XRP this means you already have been chosen. It's only a matter of time my friend.

62141467
>>62141458
I hope, but I won't allow myself to believe. And allow me to have this power at your own peril.

62141476
>>62141467
NEVER lose hope. We will achieve our wildest dreams.

62141554
How does 11 cents sound?

62141569
>>62141454
Interesting. I'd heard 589 some months ago. If it does what it sets out to then yeah even 1K seems possible in time. Pops has a bag. I'm thinking I'll be rotating gains into it since I can't put a significant amount into it right now.

62141576
>>62141554
sounds good. then I can load up my bags.

62141577
>>62141554
Like a good time to buy more

>>62141569
You did the right thing sir, please kindly invest all your money in the XRP as possible

62141906
dare i say...SO BULLISH XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62142054
>>62141454
>>62141456
>>62141457
>>62141458
>>62141464
>>62141467
>>62141476
>>62141554
I'm gonna..... I'm gonna...
IM GONNA FXCKING BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 77 replies — 9.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-04-22 05:18 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121862
I'm not sure if you guys are looking at the chart but we're really close to breaking out from the bottom
Look at picrel >>62118020 and >>62118038

62121888
>>62121769
Will it have enough marketing?
No worries my brother. I have made a marketing contract with Jeny Smith (https://thothub.mx/videos/1505023/jeny-smith-polka-dot-pantyhose/). She will market DOT for us. We are set my Mumbai brother

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121922
>>62121862
I'm not sure if you guys noticed. It's an adam and eve pattern on the 4h chart. Look at picrel

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62137662
Soon we shall reach the nirvana of 75$ my Mumbai brothers. Then we shall eat with silver spoons.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62139771
You are not the body, you are not the mind, you are not your emotions. You are the silent DOT in the infinity of consciousness. You are just a wave in the ocean of consciousness that forgot it is part of the ocean. For when rememberance dawns, the mystery is not solved. It is lived.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62141372
My brethren, the time is nigh. We're break out. The wave of consciousness is breaking free from the ocean floor.

item.title: CLEAN IT UP WAGIE CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP — 73 replies — 7.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
CLEAN IT UP WAGIE CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-04-22 03:45 UTC

OP

CLEAN IT UP WAGIE

CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

Early replies (first 3)

62119436
>>62119165
legend

62119443
Some people have almost no power over anything in their life. They do trivial evil acts like this because its their only thing they can do. Stealing stationary from work is a similar thing. His life is probably miserable.

62119490
>>62119443
Put the fries in the bag, wagie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62128026
i already told you i don't have a job
if this weren't a retard thread you'd have come up with something funnier but it's blatant vapid spam
this site is fucked

62128031
avoiding flamewarring with me to keep the post count lower & thereby keep the thread up for longer
like i said this site is fucked, everyone is way too self-aware and there are shills around every corner and the mod team treats it like a top-down rat maze with no engagement

62128032
>>62123557
here's another one

62128038
>>62119165

62128047
i'm tempted to just fill the thread with replies & see what happens

62128050
it would take hours & there'd likely be duplicate spam threads on the board within 48 hours though
makes you wonder what the mods are doing, or at least it should
>>62123557
>>62119165

62128085
>>62119443
kek

62128168
>>62128038
if you'd done a shitty mspaint trace job it would still have a soul, like pic related

62128292
>>62119165
>be me
>restaurant wagie in singapoor
>some mutt tourist think its funny to throw ketchup on the ground
>find mop and tell him to clean up
>hahaha no you are slave you do it
>call police
>he starts going off about first amendment and rights
>police gets agitatef because he doesnt do as they tell him, he argues like typical american
>they grab him to take into police car
>he starts fighting back, yelling about rights
>they taze him and beat him with police sticks
>take him to jail
I was kekking the entire time

62128310
>>62127025
Target and Trader Joe’s are can’t miss.

62130589
>>62125676
How did she manage to smuggle all that onto the flight?

62130590
>>62128292
Cope wagie. You still had to clean it up.

62130710
This thread reminded me that Kek Adams has passed.

62130818
>>62130710
Guy was such a betamale

62132756
>>62130818
Well duh, working in an office is the hallmark of betas everywhere.

62132767
>>62132756
He drew comics.
Well, you either work in an office or youre some low iq blue collar.

62132860
trickle down (onto the floor) economics.

62133126
>>62130710
>>62130818
>>62132756
weak niggas still crying about the man lmao

62133190
>>62119165
lmao americans are actually so cucked
if a customer does stupid shit we throw him out and tell him to stfu haha

62133420
>>62130710
rip

62133812
>>62128292

He was just trying to enjoy a succulent Chinese meal

62133862
>>62128038
This is an accurate depiction of the person in the image. Some people are so embarrassingly pathetic then act like they're some edgy Nihilist. You've only got this one lifetime to repent your wickedness before you meet God

62133918
Why is being in the service industry so bad in America? Like something about it is just so demeaning and people are so shitty and rude.

Being a waiter in some small village in europe would prob be chill and relaxed.

62134155
>>62119443
this, except i have complete control over my life and do it out of malice/mischief

62134162
>>62130589
You are allowed to bring any non-liquid foods onto planes.

62134229
>>62125676
What a nightmare

62137688
>>62121327
I hear the calling of all my addictions at once, fuck this is beyond demoralizing.

62139836
>>62133862
g*d must repent first

62141222
>>62125676
H...how do you tell basketball a...americans they can't eat lobster on the flight without being r...raycis...?

62141226
>>62127025
I literally fucked a chick worker at target AMA

item.title: Why do people kiss the job market's ass so badly? — 20 replies — 6.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people kiss the job market's ass so badly?

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-04-22 10:08 UTC

OP

> It's not them, IT'S YOU AND YOUR BAD RESUME BRO!
> you sent your resume to an expert already to get reviewed? YOU'RE APPLYING TO THE WRONG FIELDS BRO!
> no no no no you can't spam your resume to hundreds of locations, YOU HAVE THE TAILOR YOUR RESUME TO EACH AND EVERY JOB BRO
> not getting interviews? JUST BE A SIGMA CUM LAUDE FROM A TOP 10 UNIVERSITY BRO, JUST HAVE (CORRECT) EXPERIENCE BRO
> you got rejected after your interview? STOP BEING AN AUTIST BRO

You hear this shit literally everywhere. None of the above should ever be considered normal, but people have still normalized every job having literally 0.01% odds of you landing it with a pacific ocean sized competition pool for each of those jobs. Why do people insist that you're the problem and not the job market & corrupt HR & greedy cunt corp execs? Why do people love to kiss the "job market"'s ass so much?!

Early replies (first 3)

62138902
Applying to jobs is a numbers game. I had solid experience and two degrees, it took me a year to find another role. We’re basically in a recession with the jobs market and have been since 2023. Anyone saying you need to do this or that is coping, just keep applying and don’t be an autist during your interview.

62138903
Because if they admit the job market is ass they have to admit that they gamed the system via nepotism which indirectly tells them that they aren't as qualified as they think they are.

62138948
>>62138892
yeah its cringe same when they lick the billionaires ass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62138969
>>62138892
Because of the
>blame yourself (not others)
Psyop the jews put out there to avoid blame. Therefore anything bad happening to you is your fault. And it's a very long running psyop.

62139019
>>62138892
they're literally retarded. these are the same people who will crucify you for victim blaming a woman who was raped after dressing like a whore.

62139088
>>62138892
the problem is the job market overall isn't bad, its specifically tech. People leave this key detail out of every debate about the job market. We just have way, way, WAY too many CS graduates now, who are competing with AI and Indians for low level entry coding jobs that AI/Indians is great at. High school students need to be advised that going into tech right now is a really bad idea. Go into the trades, traditional engineering, business, etc... anything but tech.

62139121
>>62139088
>We just have way, way, WAY too many CS graduates now,
and lawyers, they're everywhere

62139130
>>62139121
>Everybody's got a lawyer and the lawyers need to make their fees!

62139133
>>62139088
>its specifically tech
Tech is just the most recent addition to the list of fields completely killed by indians and outsourcing. Look up what entry level engineers made 30-40 years ago. In the 90s starting pay for Mechanical engineers would be around 100k now.

62139185
>>62139133
There's always sanitation engineering roles available, and they'll accept you with any degree as long as you have 2 braincels to rub together.

62139356
>>62139185
mop flip mop dip is correct

62139384
>>62138892
i got the last 4 jobs i interviewed for over the last 9 years. but im a rockstar.

62139390
>>62139185
>>62139356
The right answer is dip, dip, potato chip

62139492
>>62139185
>>62139356
>>62139390
As a former fast food mopper, the answer is
>mop
>mop
>mop
>flip
>mop
>mop
>mop
>dip

62139537
>>62139492
I didn't pay enough attention and in hindsight, I realize you are indeed true in your statement.

62139686
>>62138892
Normies and psychopaths have joined forces to engage in all-out war against the autist. They harvest the negative energy we secrete when they tell us everything is fine, and any dispossession we face is a skill issue. The name of the game is maximization of mental exhaustion within the autist population.

One person slaves in a kitchen all day and makes $80, meanwhile a foid sells butthole pics and makes $800k. There's no logic you can apply to modern society.

62139734
>>62139686
Why are so many people buying butthole pics?

62140302
>>62139686
The beginning of covid was them giving back some of their advantages and losing some of their leverage to exploit us temporarily and oh did they screech.

62141809
>>62138892
You don't understand, getting work shouldn't be easy. Doing work is supposed to be the easy part.

62141826
>>62138892
It's the same as dating, a humiliation ritual. And no one wants to admit that they're participating in a humiliation ritual. So all this cope is just a rationalisation by people who think they are "hustlers" no different to guy getting surgery to looksmax so they can get a swipe from a single mother. It's all pathetic and desperate. I suggest reading Uncle Ted.

item.title: Just pull yourself up by your bootstraps — 12 replies — 5.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just pull yourself up by your bootstraps

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-22 11:04 UTC

OP

Back then:
>meet wife in college
>graduate at 22
>get married
>make enough money at 26 to hire a famous custom home builder to make you a villa in the woods near NYC
>live comfortably for the next 74 years

https://youtu.be/q5OMBYKNXW8?t=375

Today:
>childhood mindfucked by the internet
>graduate college a virgin at 22 post GFC
>tens of thousands in debt
>unemployed
>unemployed
>parents threaten to kick you out unless you work or go back to school
>graduate college a second time at 26
>still unemployed
>finally get a job
>try to be social, can't because never met any developmental milestones
>things are looking up, saving tons of cash to put a down payment on a house
>COVID 19
>years of life gone by in a flash
>everyone is mindfucked by the internet
>people retreat into hiding, avoid making eye contact, no one smiles
>young women actively hate you
>old infertile hags start to show interest and get pissed when you don't chase them
>still living with parents at 36
>burnt out, no energy to do anything outside of work except doomscroll
>down payment money turns into planning for retirement fund, just another 35 years to go

Early replies (first 3)

62141243
loser lol

62141249
>>62141240
the job market needs to improve this is just insane man

62141256
>>62141240
>Siting Boomers
Don't hate the players when the game is rigged and the rules change the later you play.
>What used to work for them hasn't worked or been relevant for decades.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141262
>>62141256
He's 100 years old, he predates even the boomers that bought suburban cookie cutter houses. He lived in a time where you could just do whatever the fuck you wanted like building a hippie commune in the woods without the government razing it to the ground and murdering your wife and son.

62141329
>>62141240
We have some things in common but totally different outcome
>childhood mindfucked by the internet
>graduate high school and quit RuneScape cold turkey
>go to college and make friends, party, and do drugs
>graduate college a virgin at 22 post GFC
>"only" thousands in debt because of HOPE scholarship and grants
>accepted to post-graduate program
>work while in school and graduate at age 26
>$150k+ in student loan debt
>immediately promoted from intern to job paying $110k
>move out of parents place and get cheap apartment
>try to get gf but too focused on making money
>discover crypto in early 2017 and eventually make enough to pay off student loans
>COVID 19
>happy to stop going out with almost all friends because of lockdown
>annual income approaching $200k
>investments really paying off and interest rates in the toilet so I buy a house and new Tesla
>NW reaches $1 million
>get practice gf
>dump practice gf
>NW reaches $2 million
>get new gf 10+ years younger than me
>quit job and move to Thailand at age 36

62141336
>>62141256
I'm going to sue my mom so she literally dies in prison because she committed fraud against me. I will 'hate the player' and fags like you can sob and cry on behalf of someone else's terrible parents.

62141381
>>62141329
Based, way to save the thread. I'm sick of complainers. They're not wrong but what a way to spend your life.

62141547
>>62141329
Finally get a trad wife achievement unlocked.

62141688
>>62141240
Today:
>10-hour days at Amazon
>browse biz and do my own research like they say
>multiply investments by 10, 100, even 1000
>take time to join spaces where people share my interests (even online)
>communicate with punctuation instead of spouting memes and innuendos constantly
>get interest and confessions from decent people without ever expecting it
>cutie apologizing for possibly being too bold inviting me to come hang out and even go spelunking someday
This is all after lockdowns. Researching what to invest in is boring and even mind-melting early on, but in many ways life revolves around learning, whether it's a new workplace and skills, or a new acquaintance and their interests.
Like me, you don't have to be right every single time. There are opportunities ahead you can't see and that don't even exist today. Best dip I ever spotted didn't exist until months after I first began looking for stuff to invest in. Do I see everything? Not at all. ASTEROID sat at such an ideal price since January and I only heard about it a few days ago after it popped off.
OP you are definitely capable of learning and growing. You actually did college unlike me who didn't even bother with grade 12 math. Just keep your eye out for opportunity. I could've made it much sooner if I took bigger chances sooner, but even that first time I chose well and a mere 1K turned into 30K a year later, has ended up being a story I use to encourage my friends to invest and believe in their ability to learn.
Multiple times this has resulted rather surprisingly in even girls I've been talking to for a while showing some admiration. Even better, because of explaining it in a way that shows it's just a matter of learning, sometimes they ask if I think they can succeed too, or ask if I have any advice on getting started. Rise up OP and keep your eye out for opportunity.

62141794
>>62141240
>always feel like need to shit
>never have anything to shit out

62141912
>>62141688
sar i hope you paid many of ruppee for these post

62141916
>>62141256
The boomers are the ones who made the current rules of the game.

>>62141262
You can't even put a caravan / mobile home on a bit of land you own in the UK without local government approval (which will be denied).

item.title: How much BTC you holding right now? — 102 replies — 4.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC you holding right now?

Open thread • Replies: 102 • Updated: 2026-04-21 16:33 UTC

OP

How much BTC do you currently have in your wallet, and what’s your target amount to hold before it reaches $1M?

>I mean, if you don’t even have at least 1 BTC and you’re in crypto, it still feels pointless.

Early replies (first 3)

62114988
>>62114930
1.03

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62126533
>>62114930
https://timesofmalta.com/article/elusive-bitcoin-founder-adam-back-lives-malta.1126648
What are the financial implications of this?

62126561
>>62123814
What risk are you talking about here? Also, it’s better now that there are returns instead of just holding with no yield. This setup is way better overall.

62126610
>>62117395
but why did you sell?

62126617
>>62117832
buy the bottom? but at which price?

62127919
>>62124487
More!!!

62128353
>>62120218
YOU CAN DO IT BOI

62128654
>>62125560
Don't worry about it. Keep going and have fun. YOLO.

62130077
>>62122141
Why FUD

62130099
0.3

62131443
>>62130099
Just 0.3? Make it whole bitcoin

62131447
>>62126507
since the COVID boom? It was just $8k during covid

62131531
>>62114930
The new paradigm is no more peaks.

62131569
>>62114930
Zero and zero

62132043
1.72 bitcoins
*sigh*
I keep stacking, maybe one day, I'll have 2 whole bitcoins.

62132222
around 10, i might end up keeping them forever as a backup plan. Looking at my taxes my average accumulation price was 29k and sell price was 109k Tidy little 70k each. I'll buy back in when the dump is over and it settles between 20-30k. for supposedly being a crypto board you all sure seem to struggle with basic pattern recognition, this is exactly what happens every time, dump 75% from the peak and then the next bull run starts.

62132243
>>62115773
I remember that 21 used to be the suistack. Everyone's goal was to accumulate AT LEAST 21 btc.

62132565
>>62131531
Why?

62133350
>>62131447
61k in March 2021.

62133953
>>62131569
That's bad.

62136283
>>62133350
I saw the chart

62136286
>>62132043
You can always make it. Start small

62136448
>>62116911
>I’m actually selling all my crypto this week. Cope and seethe
Doing a lot of cope and seething huh?

62136454
>>62115225
>Macbook
You might as well shared your dildo collection anon.

62137580
>>62136454
>not using the most cost-effective general purpose laptop in 2026
Yeah, you're ngmi

62138430
>>62136448
Why did you do that ?

62138432
>>62136454
lmao

62138468
Had several BTC and loads of ETH back in college when I sorted fake ids for my friends, lost it all on a physical flash drive wallet. BTC was only worth ~$20k a coin so I'm not at the point of offing myself over losses yet lmao

62138538
>>62138468
That was your fault tho.

62138592
>>62138538
Yup, got panicked and boxed up all that bs when someone reported me to the RA. Thought I eventually packed it with me when I moved, probably got nicked by someone. Good times.

62138882
I basically missed every pump and now I hold onto 0.35 BTC and I’m never selling it, ever. I sometimes get really mad that I had 15 at one point, could’ve easily had more but I let every so-called “export” and fudder convince me it was worthless. I could’ve been retired at the age of 21 and now I gotta wage it out until I’m 65 assuming AI doesn’t kill us.

item.title: /GRT/ The Graph — 34 replies — 3.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
/GRT/ The Graph

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-04-22 03:38 UTC

OP

Is it truly over GRTbros?

Will our GRTies ever come back from obscurity? Or is it truly lost?

Early replies (first 3)

62128193
>>62128177
MY GRT GRT GRT MAKES MY FELLOW TRANNIES SQUIRT SQUIRT SQUIRT

62128195
>>62128193
...we deserve the hell we're in

62128200
>>62128195
100% Bitcoin you nigger.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62128208
>>62128202
...like GRT? you dumb fuck

62128224
>>62128208
>...like GRT?
Sure why the fuck not

62128247
It's over wasn't just a meme. If you didn't sell in 2021, you're a retard.

62128257
>>62128247
I did sell in 2021

I'm just thinking it's about time to jump back in

It's probably gonna start rallying up against Bitcoin now

62128260
>>62128177
There is no difference between this baggie and a million other different alt coin baggies. Most alts are going to zero. Sorry not sorry.

62128262
>>62128260
Nah, GRT is built different

62128269
>>62128262
Yes and another 2021 rally is totally coming back again for digital nothing pixels. What makes your ponzi any different from the next ? Kek Baggies.

62128282
>>62128269
I guess we'll just have to wait and see who ends up on top

In about a year GRT will reach 2021 highs and I will be laffin'

62128644
GRT is going to $9.99. GRT, AMP and JASMY are Kek's chosen coins.

62128731
>>62128247
Too red eye, bless up rassclat. Yuns nuh wut da papa be singing! Ya no call dem ‘gaters long mouths till ya pass dem. We booyaka Babylon in due tim mon. Big up ya self natty dreads, when chicken marry, hawk dey near. But I be a broken record. They ai dat web3 wooooooo, faze me. Jah guide.

62129571
>>62128731
Rasta anon is back!

62129610
Sold at a loss for a tax write off.
Silver linings and all that.

62129655
>>62128177
Shit's dead. Turns out the Google of blockchains was the AskJeeves of blockchains. So pissed at myself for buying the top at 37 cents.

62129743
>>62129655
>Shit's dead
For now, but we're probably at the generational bottom right now
It's the time to slurp up cheapies

62129969
No one uses graph databases anymore. Why would anyone use one with a blockchain attached?

62130358
>>62129969
>Midwit take
TheGraph is not a graph database

62130457
>>62129610
I'd start to buy back in if I were you

62131285
It's been over for years

62133372
>>62130358
Dem bullheads tink a chart is a graph. Bun weed. Everything is Everthing is everywhere all da tim as one. Jah bless.

62133566
>>62131285
Maybe it's been so long that it's unovered over and now it's no longer over?

You ever thought 'bout that? Huh?

62133917
>>62133372
>>62128731
Sold everything. Enough is enough.

62133964
The Scam

62134608
>>62133917
If rasta anon makes you sell, you were ngmi in the first place

62134612
>>62133964
It's not just any scam
It's the Duck Duck Go of scams!

62136329
>>62134608
I don't like niggers simple as
GURT is a NIGGER coin confirmed
t. 50k of this shit delegated and too lazy to sell

62136450
>>62136329
>GURT is a NIGGER coin confirmed
Mayhaps
It'll still lead the charge on altcoins, though

62136774
>>62128644
I sold Jasmy at that big November peak when Binance listed it a few years back and bought a big comfy mattress with it. I literally sleep on that coin

62140982
>>62136329
Mah GURTs are no niggercoin, good sir

I'd appreciate if you apologized to the coin

It's a decent coin, no nigger business here

62141121
>>62136450

62141214
>>62141121
